NKJV.1: The king’s heart is in the hand of the LORD, Like the rivers of water; He turns it wherever He wishes.
NKJV.2: Every way of a man is right in his own eyes, But the LORD weighs the hearts.
NKJV.3: To do righteousness and justice Is more acceptable to the LORD than sacrifice.
NKJV.4: A haughty look, a proud heart, And the plowing of the wicked are sin.
NKJV.5: The plans of the diligent lead surely to plenty, But those of everyone who is hasty, surely to poverty.
NKJV.6: Getting treasures by a lying tongue Is the fleeting fantasy of those who seek death.
NKJV.7: The violence of the wicked will destroy them, Because they refuse to do justice.
NKJV.8: The way of a guilty man is perverse; But as for the pure, his work is right.
NKJV.9: Better to dwell in a corner of a housetop, Than in a house shared with a contentious woman.
NKJV.10: The soul of the wicked desires evil; His neighbor finds no favor in his eyes.
NKJV.11: When the scoffer is punished, the simple is made wise; But when the wise is instructed, he receives knowledge.
NKJV.12: The righteous God wisely considers the house of the wicked, Overthrowing the wicked for their wickedness.
NKJV.13: Whoever shuts his ears to the cry of the poor Will also cry himself and not be heard.
NKJV.14: A gift in secret pacifies anger, And a bribe behind the back, strong wrath.
NKJV.15: It is a joy for the just to do justice, But destruction will come to the workers of iniquity.
NKJV.16: A man who wanders from the way of understanding Will rest in the assembly of the dead.
NKJV.17: He who loves pleasure will be a poor man; He who loves wine and oil will not be rich.
NKJV.18: The wicked shall be a ransom for the righteous, And the unfaithful for the upright.
NKJV.19: Better to dwell in the wilderness, Than with a contentious and angry woman.
NKJV.20: There is desirable treasure, And oil in the dwelling of the wise, But a foolish man squanders it.
NKJV.21: He who follows righteousness and mercy Finds life, righteousness, and honor.
NKJV.22: A wise man scales the city of the mighty, And brings down the trusted stronghold.
NKJV.23: Whoever guards his mouth and tongue Keeps his soul from troubles.
NKJV.24: A proud and haughty man— “Scoffer” is his name; He acts with arrogant pride.
NKJV.25: The desire of the lazy man kills him, For his hands refuse to labor.
NKJV.26: He covets greedily all day long, But the righteous gives and does not spare.
NKJV.27: The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination; How much more when he brings it with wicked intent!
NKJV.28: A false witness shall perish, But the man who hears him will speak endlessly.
NKJV.29: A wicked man hardens his face, But as for the upright, he establishes his way.
NKJV.30: There is no wisdom or understanding Or counsel against the LORD.
NKJV.31: The horse is prepared for the day of battle, But deliverance is of the LORD.

KJV.9: It is better<S>2896</S> to dwell<S>3427</S> in a corner<S>6438</S> of the housetop<S>1406</S>, than with a brawling<S>4079</S> <S>4066</S> woman<S>802</S> in a wide<S>2267</S> house<S>1004</S>. <sup>a brawling: Heb. a woman of contentions</sup> <sup>a wide: Heb. an house of society</sup>

ESV.1: The king's heart is a stream of water in the hand of the LORD; he turns it wherever he will.
ESV.2: Every way of a man is right in his own eyes, but the LORD weighs the heart.
ESV.3: To do righteousness and justice is more acceptable to the LORD than sacrifice.
ESV.4: Haughty eyes and a proud heart, the lamp  of the wicked, are sin.
ESV.5: The plans of the diligent lead surely to abundance, but everyone who is hasty comes only to poverty.
ESV.6: The getting of treasures by a lying tongue is a fleeting vapor and a snare of death.
ESV.7: The violence of the wicked will sweep them away, because they refuse to do what is just.
ESV.8: The way of the guilty is crooked, but the conduct of the pure is upright.
ESV.9: It is better to live in a corner of the housetop than in a house shared with a quarrelsome wife.
ESV.10: The soul of the wicked desires evil; his neighbor finds no mercy in his eyes.
ESV.11: When a scoffer is punished, the simple becomes wise; when a wise man is instructed, he gains knowledge.
ESV.12: The Righteous One observes the house of the wicked; he throws the wicked down to ruin.
ESV.13: Whoever closes his ear to the cry of the poor will himself call out and not be answered.
ESV.14: A gift in secret averts anger, and a concealed bribe,  strong wrath.
ESV.15: When justice is done, it is a joy to the righteous but terror to evildoers.
ESV.16: One who wanders from the way of good sense will rest in the assembly of the dead.
ESV.17: Whoever loves pleasure will be a poor man; he who loves wine and oil will not be rich.
ESV.18: The wicked is a ransom for the righteous, and the traitor for the upright.
ESV.19: It is better to live in a desert land than with a quarrelsome and fretful woman.
ESV.20: Precious treasure and oil are in a wise man's dwelling, but a foolish man devours it.
ESV.21: Whoever pursues righteousness and kindness will find life, righteousness, and honor.
ESV.22: A wise man scales the city of the mighty and brings down the stronghold in which they trust.
ESV.23: Whoever keeps his mouth and his tongue keeps himself out of trouble.
ESV.24: “Scoffer” is the name of the arrogant, haughty man who acts with arrogant pride.
ESV.25: The desire of the sluggard kills him, for his hands refuse to labor.
ESV.26: All day long he craves and craves, but the righteous gives and does not hold back.
ESV.27: The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination; how much more when he brings it with evil intent.
ESV.28: A false witness will perish, but the word of a man who hears will endure.
ESV.29: A wicked man puts on a bold face, but the upright gives thought to  his ways.
ESV.30: No wisdom, no understanding, no counsel can avail against the LORD.
ESV.31: The horse is made ready for the day of battle, but the victory belongs to the LORD.

NASB.1: The king's heart is [like] channels of water in the hand of the LORD; He turns it wherever He wishes.
NASB.2: Every man's way is right in his own eyes, But the LORD weighs the hearts.
NASB.3: To do righteousness and justice Is desired by the LORD more than sacrifice.
NASB.4: Haughty eyes and a proud heart, The lamp of the wicked, is sin.
NASB.5: The plans of the diligent [lead] surely to advantage, But everyone who is hasty [comes] surely to poverty.
NASB.6: The acquisition of treasures by a lying tongue Is a fleeting vapor, the pursuit of death.
NASB.7: The violence of the wicked will drag them away, Because they refuse to act with justice.
NASB.8: The way of a guilty man is crooked, But as for the pure, his conduct is upright.
NASB.9: It is better to live in a corner of a roof Than in a house shared with a contentious woman.
NASB.10: The soul of the wicked desires evil; His neighbor finds no favor in his eyes.
NASB.11: When the scoffer is punished, the naive becomes wise; But when the wise is instructed, he receives knowledge.
NASB.12: The righteous one considers the house of the wicked, Turning the wicked to ruin.
NASB.13: He who shuts his ear to the cry of the poor Will also cry himself and not be answered.
NASB.14: A gift in secret subdues anger, And a bribe in the bosom, strong wrath.
NASB.15: The exercise of justice is joy for the righteous, But is terror to the workers of iniquity.
NASB.16: A man who wanders from the way of understanding Will rest in the assembly of the dead.
NASB.17: He who loves pleasure [will become] a poor man; He who loves wine and oil will not become rich.
NASB.18: The wicked is a ransom for the righteous, And the treacherous is in the place of the upright.
NASB.19: It is better to live in a desert land Than with a contentious and vexing woman.
NASB.20: There is precious treasure and oil in the dwelling of the wise, But a foolish man swallows it up.
NASB.21: He who pursues righteousness and loyalty Finds life, righteousness and honor.
NASB.22: A wise man scales the city of the mighty And brings down the stronghold in which they trust.
NASB.23: He who guards his mouth and his tongue, Guards his soul from troubles.
NASB.24: 'Proud,' 'Haughty,' 'Scoffer,' are his names, Who acts with insolent pride.
NASB.25: The desire of the sluggard puts him to death, For his hands refuse to work;
NASB.26: All day long he is craving, While the righteous gives and does not hold back.
NASB.27: The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination, How much more when he brings it with evil intent!
NASB.28: A false witness will perish, But the man who listens [to the truth] will speak forever.
NASB.29: A wicked man displays a bold face, But as for the upright, he makes his way sure.
NASB.30: There is no wisdom and no understanding And no counsel against the LORD.
NASB.31: The horse is prepared for the day of battle, But victory belongs to the LORD.

NLT.1:
The king’s heart is like a stream of water directed by the LORD;
he guides it wherever he pleases.
NLT.2:
People may be right in their own eyes,
but the LORD examines their heart.
NLT.3:
The LORD is more pleased when we do what is right and just
than when we offer him sacrifices.
NLT.4:
Haughty eyes, a proud heart,
and evil actions are all sin.
NLT.5:
Good planning and hard work lead to prosperity,
but hasty shortcuts lead to poverty.
NLT.6:
Wealth created by a lying tongue
is a vanishing mist and a deadly trap.
NLT.7:
The violence of the wicked sweeps them away,
because they refuse to do what is just.
NLT.8:
The guilty walk a crooked path;
the innocent travel a straight road.
NLT.9:
It’s better to live alone in the corner of an attic
than with a quarrelsome wife in a lovely home.
NLT.10:
Evil people desire evil;
their neighbors get no mercy from them.
NLT.11:
If you punish a mocker, the simpleminded become wise;
if you instruct the wise, they will be all the wiser.
NLT.12:
The Righteous One knows what is going on in the homes of the wicked;
he will bring disaster on them.
NLT.13:
Those who shut their ears to the cries of the poor
will be ignored in their own time of need.
NLT.14:
A secret gift calms anger;
a bribe under the table pacifies fury.
NLT.15:
Justice is a joy to the godly,
but it terrifies evildoers.
NLT.16:
The person who strays from common sense
will end up in the company of the dead.
NLT.17:
Those who love pleasure become poor;
those who love wine and luxury will never be rich.
NLT.18:
The wicked are punished in place of the godly,
and traitors in place of the honest.
NLT.19:
It’s better to live alone in the desert
than with a quarrelsome, complaining wife.
NLT.20:
The wise have wealth and luxury,
but fools spend whatever they get.
NLT.21:
Whoever pursues righteousness and unfailing love
will find life, righteousness, and honor.
NLT.22:
The wise conquer the city of the strong
and level the fortress in which they trust.
NLT.23:
Watch your tongue and keep your mouth shut,
and you will stay out of trouble.
NLT.24:
Mockers are proud and haughty;
they act with boundless arrogance.
NLT.25:
Despite their desires, the lazy will come to ruin,
for their hands refuse to work.
NLT.26:
Some people are always greedy for more,
but the godly love to give!
NLT.27:
The sacrifice of an evil person is detestable,
especially when it is offered with wrong motives.
NLT.28:
A false witness will be cut off,
but a credible witness will be allowed to speak.
NLT.29:
The wicked bluff their way through,
but the virtuous think before they act.
NLT.30:
No human wisdom or understanding or plan
can stand against the LORD.
NLT.31:
The horse is prepared for the day of battle,
but the victory belongs to the LORD.

WEB.1: The king’s heart is in Yahweh’s hand like the watercourses. He turns it wherever he desires.
WEB.2: Every way of a man is right in his own eyes, but Yahweh weighs the hearts.
WEB.3: To do righteousness and justice is more acceptable to Yahweh than sacrifice.
WEB.4: A high look, and a proud heart, the lamp of the wicked, is sin.
WEB.5: The plans of the diligent surely lead to profit; and everyone who is hasty surely rushes to poverty.
WEB.6: Getting treasures by a lying tongue is a fleeting vapor for those who seek death.
WEB.7: The violence of the wicked will drive them away, because they refuse to do what is right.
WEB.8: The way of the guilty is devious, but the conduct of the innocent is upright.
WEB.9: It is better to dwell in the corner of the housetop, than to share a house with a contentious woman.
WEB.10: The soul of the wicked desires evil; his neighbor finds no mercy in his eyes.
WEB.11: When the mocker is punished, the simple gains wisdom. When the wise is instructed, he receives knowledge.
WEB.12: The Righteous One considers the house of the wicked, and brings the wicked to ruin.
WEB.13: Whoever stops his ears at the cry of the poor, he will also cry out, but shall not be heard.
WEB.14: A gift in secret pacifies anger; and a bribe in the cloak, strong wrath.
WEB.15: It is joy to the righteous to do justice; but it is a destruction to the workers of iniquity.
WEB.16: The man who wanders out of the way of understanding shall rest in the assembly of the departed spirits.
WEB.17: He who loves pleasure shall be a poor man. He who loves wine and oil shall not be rich.
WEB.18: The wicked is a ransom for the righteous; the treacherous for the upright.
WEB.19: It is better to dwell in a desert land, than with a contentious and fretful woman.
WEB.20: There is precious treasure and oil in the dwelling of the wise; but a foolish man swallows it up.
WEB.21: He who follows after righteousness and kindness finds life, righteousness, and honor.
WEB.22: A wise man scales the city of the mighty, and brings down the strength of its confidence.
WEB.23: Whoever guards his mouth and his tongue keeps his soul from troubles.
WEB.24: The proud and haughty man, “scoffer” is his name; he works in the arrogance of pride.
WEB.25: The desire of the sluggard kills him, for his hands refuse to labor.
WEB.26: There are those who covet greedily all day long; but the righteous give and don’t withhold.
WEB.27: The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination; how much more, when he brings it with a wicked mind!
WEB.28: A false witness will perish, and a man who listens speaks to eternity.
WEB.29: A wicked man hardens his face; but as for the upright, he establishes his ways.
WEB.30: There is no wisdom nor understanding nor counsel against Yahweh.
WEB.31: The horse is prepared for the day of battle; but victory is with Yahweh.

CSB17.1:
A king’s heart is like channeled water in the LORD’s hand:
He directs it wherever he chooses. ⓘ
CSB17.2:
All a person’s ways seem right to him,
but the LORD weighs hearts. ⓙ
CSB17.3:
Doing what is righteous and just
is more acceptable to the LORD than sacrifice. ⓚ
CSB17.4:
The lamp that guides the wicked —
haughty eyes and an arrogant heart ⓛ  — is sin.
CSB17.5:
The plans of the diligent certainly lead to profit,
but anyone who is reckless certainly becomes poor. ⓜ
CSB17.6:
Making a fortune through a lying tongue
is a vanishing mist, [1] a pursuit of death. [2] [3] ⓝ
CSB17.7:
The violence of the wicked sweeps them away
because they refuse to act justly.
CSB17.8:
A guilty one’s conduct is crooked, ⓐ
but the behavior of the innocent is upright. ⓑ
CSB17.9:
Better to live on the corner of a roof
than to share a house with a nagging wife. ⓒ
CSB17.10:
A wicked person desires evil;
he has no consideration [4] for his neighbor. ⓓ
CSB17.11:
When a mocker is punished,
the inexperienced become wiser;
when one teaches a wise man,
he acquires knowledge. ⓔ
CSB17.12:
The Righteous One [5] considers the house of the wicked;
he brings the wicked to ruin.
CSB17.13:
The one who shuts his ears to the cry of the poor
will himself also call out and not be answered. ⓕ
CSB17.14:
A secret gift soothes anger,
and a covert bribe, fierce rage. ⓖ
CSB17.15:
Justice executed is a joy to the righteous
but a terror to those who practice iniquity. ⓗ
CSB17.16:
The person who strays from the way of prudence
will come to rest in the assembly of the departed spirits. ⓘ
CSB17.17:
The one who loves pleasure will become poor;
whoever loves wine and oil will not get rich. ⓙ
CSB17.18:
The wicked are a ransom for the righteous,
and the treacherous, for [6] the upright. ⓚ
CSB17.19:
Better to live in a wilderness
than with a nagging and hot-tempered wife. ⓛ
CSB17.20:
Precious treasure and oil are in the dwelling of a wise person,
but a fool consumes them. [7] ⓜ
CSB17.21:
The one who pursues righteousness and faithful love
will find life, righteousness, and honor. ⓝ
CSB17.22:
A wise person went up against a city of warriors
and brought down its secure fortress. ⓐ
CSB17.23:
The one who guards his mouth and tongue
keeps himself out of trouble. ⓑ
CSB17.24:
The arrogant and proud person, named “Mocker,”
acts with excessive arrogance. ⓒ
CSB17.25:
A slacker’s craving will kill him
because his hands refuse to work. ⓓ
CSB17.26:
He is filled with craving [8] all day long,
but the righteous give and don’t hold back. ⓔ
CSB17.27:
The sacrifice of a wicked person is detestable —
how much more so
when he brings it with ulterior motives! ⓕ
CSB17.28:
A lying witness will perish, ⓖ
but the one who listens will speak successfully.
CSB17.29:
A wicked person puts on a bold face, ⓗ
but the upright one considers his way.
CSB17.30:
No wisdom, no understanding, and no counsel
will prevail against the LORD. ⓘ
CSB17.31:
A horse is prepared for the day of battle, ⓙ
but victory comes from the LORD. ⓚ

AMP.1:
The king's heart is like channels of water in the hand of the LORD;
He turns it whichever way He wishes.
AMP.2:
Every man's way is right in his own eyes,
But the LORD weighs and examines the hearts [of people and their motives].
AMP.3:
To do righteousness and justice
Is more acceptable to the LORD than sacrifice [for wrongs repeatedly committed].
AMP.4:
Haughty and arrogant eyes and a proud heart,
The lamp of the wicked [their self-centered pride], is sin [in the eyes of God].
AMP.5:
The plans of the diligent lead surely to abundance and advantage,
But everyone who acts in haste comes surely to poverty.
AMP.6:
Acquiring treasures by a lying tongue
Is a fleeting vapor, the seeking and pursuit of death.
AMP.7:
The violence of the wicked will [return to them and] drag them away [like fish caught in a net],
Because they refuse to act with justice.
AMP.8:
The way of the guilty is [exceedingly] crooked,
But as for the pure, his conduct is upright.
AMP.9:
It is better to live in a corner of the housetop [on the flat roof, exposed to the weather]
Than in a house shared with a quarrelsome (contentious) woman.
AMP.10:
The soul of the wicked desires evil [like an addictive substance];
His neighbor finds no compassion in his eyes.
AMP.11:
When the scoffer is punished, the naive [observes the lesson and] becomes wise;
But when the wise and teachable person is instructed, he receives knowledge.
AMP.12:
The righteous one keeps an eye on the house of the wicked—
How the wicked are cast down to ruin.
AMP.13:
Whoever shuts his ears at the cry of the poor
Will cry out himself and not be answered.
AMP.14:
A gift in secret subdues anger,
And a bribe [hidden] in the pocket, strong wrath.
AMP.15:
When justice is done, it is a joy to the righteous (the upright, the one in right standing with God),
But to the evildoers it is disaster.
AMP.16:
A man who wanders from the way of understanding (godly wisdom)
Will remain in the assembly of the dead.
AMP.17:
He who loves [only selfish] pleasure will become a poor man;
He who loves and is devoted to wine and [olive] oil will not become rich.
AMP.18:
The wicked become a ransom for the righteous,
And the treacherous in the place of the upright [for they fall into their own traps].
AMP.19:
It is better to dwell in a desert land
Than with a contentious and troublesome woman.
AMP.20:
There is precious treasure and oil in the house of the wise [who prepare for the future],
But a short-sighted and foolish man swallows it up and wastes it.
AMP.21:
He who earnestly seeks righteousness and loyalty
Finds life, righteousness, and honor.
AMP.22:
A wise man scales the city [walls] of the mighty
And brings down the stronghold in which they trust.
AMP.23:
He who guards his mouth and his tongue
Guards himself from troubles.
AMP.24:
"Proud," "Haughty," "Scoffer," are his names
Who acts with overbearing and insolent pride.
AMP.25:
The desire of the lazy kills him,
For his hands refuse to labor;
AMP.26:
He craves all the day long [and does no work],
But the righteous [willingly] gives and does not withhold [what he has].
AMP.27:
The sacrifice of the wicked is detestable and offensive [to the LORD].
How much more [unacceptable and insulting can it be] when he brings it with evil intention?
AMP.28:
A false witness will perish,
But a man who listens to the truth will speak forever and go unchallenged.
AMP.29:
A wicked man puts on a bold face,
But as for the upright, he considers, directs, and establishes his way [with the confidence of integrity].
AMP.30:
There is no [human] wisdom or understanding
Or counsel [that can prevail] against the LORD.
AMP.31:
The horse is prepared for the day of battle,
But deliverance and victory belong to the LORD.

MSG.1:
Good leadership is a channel of water controlled by God;
he directs it to whatever ends he chooses.
MSG.2:
We justify our actions by appearances;
God examines our motives.
MSG.3:
Clean living before God and justice with our neighbors
mean far more to God than religious performance.
MSG.4:
Arrogance and pride—distinguishing marks in the wicked—
are just plain sin.
MSG.5:
Careful planning puts you ahead in the long run;
hurry and scurry puts you further behind.
MSG.6:
Make it to the top by lying and cheating;
get paid with smoke and a promotion—to death!
MSG.7:
The wicked get buried alive by their loot
because they refuse to use it to help others.
MSG.8:
Mixed motives twist life into tangles;
pure motives take you straight down the road.
MSG.9:
Better to live alone in a tumbledown shack
than share a mansion with a nagging spouse.
MSG.10:
Wicked souls love to make trouble;
they feel nothing for friends and neighbors.
MSG.11:
Simpletons only learn the hard way,
but the wise learn by listening.
MSG.12:
A God-loyal person will see right through the wicked
and undo the evil they've planned.
MSG.13:
If you stop your ears to the cries of the poor,
your cries will go unheard, unanswered.
MSG.14:
A quietly given gift soothes an irritable person;
a heartfelt present cools a hot temper.
MSG.15:
Good people celebrate when justice triumphs,
but for the workers of evil it's a bad day.
MSG.16:
Whoever wanders off the straight and narrow
ends up in a congregation of ghosts.
MSG.17:
You're addicted to thrills? What an empty life!
The pursuit of pleasure is never satisfied.
MSG.18:
What a bad person plots against the good, boomerangs;
the plotter gets it in the end.
MSG.19:
Better to live in a tent in the wild
than with a cross and petulant spouse.
MSG.20:
Valuables are safe in a wise person's home;
fools put it all out for yard sales.
MSG.21:
Whoever goes hunting for what is right and kind
finds life itself—glorious life!
MSG.22:
One sage entered a whole city of armed soldiers—
their trusted defenses fell to pieces!
MSG.23:
Watch your words and hold your tongue;
you'll save yourself a lot of grief.
MSG.24:
You know their names—Brash, Impudent, Blasphemer—
intemperate hotheads, every one.
MSG.25:
Lazy people finally die of hunger
because they won't get up and go to work.
MSG.26:
Sinners are always wanting what they don't have;
the God-loyal are always giving what they do have.
MSG.27:
Religious performance by the wicked stinks;
it's even worse when they use it to get ahead.
MSG.28:
A lying witness is unconvincing;
a person who speaks truth is respected.
MSG.29:
Unscrupulous people fake it a lot;
honest people are sure of their steps.
MSG.30:
Nothing clever, nothing conceived, nothing contrived,
can get the better of God.
MSG.31:
Do your best, prepare for the worst—
then trust God to bring victory.

NIV.1:
The king’s heart is in the hand of the Lord;
he directs it like a watercourse wherever he pleases.
NIV.2:
All a man’s ways seem right to him,
but the Lord weighs the heart.
NIV.3:
To do what is right and just
is more acceptable to the Lord than sacrifice.
NIV.4:
Haughty eyes and a proud heart,
the lamp of the wicked, are sin!
NIV.5:
The plans of the diligent lead to profit
as surely as haste leads to poverty.
NIV.6:
A fortune made by a lying tongue
is a fleeting vapor and a deadly snare.
NIV.7:
The violence of the wicked will drag them away,
for they refuse to do what is right.
NIV.8:
The way of the guilty is devious,
but the conduct of the innocent is upright.
NIV.9:
Better to live on a corner of the roof
than share a house with a quarrelsome wife.
NIV.10:
The wicked man craves evil;
his neighbor gets no mercy from him.
NIV.11:
When a mocker is punished, the simple gain wisdom;
when a wise man is instructed, he gets knowledge.
NIV.12:
The Righteous One takes note of the house of the wicked
and brings the wicked to ruin.
NIV.13:
If a man shuts his ears to the cry of the poor,
he too will cry out and not be answered.
NIV.14:
A gift given in secret soothes anger,
and a bribe concealed in the cloak pacifies great wrath.
NIV.15:
When justice is done, it brings joy to the righteous
but terror to evildoers.
NIV.16:
A man who strays from the path of understanding
comes to rest in the company of the dead.
NIV.17:
He who loves pleasure will become poor;
whoever loves wine and oil will never be rich.
NIV.18:
The wicked become a ransom for the righteous,
and the unfaithful for the upright.
NIV.19:
Better to live in a desert
than with a quarrelsome and ill-tempered wife.
NIV.20:
In the house of the wise are stores of choice food and oil,
but a foolish man devours all he has.
NIV.21:
He who pursues righteousness and love
finds life, prosperity and honor.
NIV.22:
A wise man attacks the city of the mighty
and pulls down the stronghold in which they trust.
NIV.23:
He who guards his mouth and his tongue
keeps himself from calamity.
NIV.24:
The proud and arrogant man—“Mocker” is his name;
he behaves with overweening pride.
NIV.25:
The sluggard’s craving will be the death of him,
because his hands refuse to work.
NIV.26:
All day long he craves for more,
but the righteous give without sparing.
NIV.27:
The sacrifice of the wicked is detestable—
how much more so when brought with evil intent!
NIV.28:
A false witness will perish,
and whoever listens to him will be destroyed forever.
NIV.29:
A wicked man puts up a bold front,
but an upright man gives thought to his ways.
NIV.30:
There is no wisdom, no insight, no plan
that can succeed against the Lord.
NIV.31:
The horse is made ready for the day of battle,
but victory rests with the Lord.

NRSVCE.1:
The king’s heart is a stream of water in the hand of the LORD;
he turns it wherever he will.
NRSVCE.2:
All deeds are right in the sight of the doer,
but the LORD weighs the heart.
NRSVCE.3:
To do righteousness and justice
is more acceptable to the LORD than sacrifice.
NRSVCE.4:
Haughty eyes and a proud heart —
the lamp of the wicked — are sin.
NRSVCE.5:
The plans of the diligent lead surely to abundance,
but everyone who is hasty comes only to want.
NRSVCE.6:
The getting of treasures by a lying tongue
is a fleeting vapor and a snare of death.
NRSVCE.7:
The violence of the wicked will sweep them away,
because they refuse to do what is just.
NRSVCE.8:
The way of the guilty is crooked,
but the conduct of the pure is right.
NRSVCE.9:
It is better to live in a corner of the housetop
than in a house shared with a contentious wife.
NRSVCE.10:
The souls of the wicked desire evil;
their neighbors find no mercy in their eyes.
NRSVCE.11:
When a scoffer is punished, the simple become wiser;
when the wise are instructed, they increase in knowledge.
NRSVCE.12:
The Righteous One observes the house of the wicked;
he casts the wicked down to ruin.
NRSVCE.13:
If you close your ear to the cry of the poor,
you will cry out and not be heard.
NRSVCE.14:
A gift in secret averts anger;
and a concealed bribe in the bosom, strong wrath.
NRSVCE.15:
When justice is done, it is a joy to the righteous,
but dismay to evildoers.
NRSVCE.16:
Whoever wanders from the way of understanding
will rest in the assembly of the dead.
NRSVCE.17:
Whoever loves pleasure will suffer want;
whoever loves wine and oil will not be rich.
NRSVCE.18:
The wicked is a ransom for the righteous,
and the faithless for the upright.
NRSVCE.19:
It is better to live in a desert land
than with a contentious and fretful wife.
NRSVCE.20:
Precious treasure remains in the house of the wise,
but the fool devours it.
NRSVCE.21:
Whoever pursues righteousness and kindness
will find life and honor.
NRSVCE.22:
One wise person went up against a city of warriors
and brought down the stronghold in which they trusted.
NRSVCE.23:
To watch over mouth and tongue
is to keep out of trouble.
NRSVCE.24:
The proud, haughty person, named “Scoffer,”
acts with arrogant pride.
NRSVCE.25:
The craving of the lazy person is fatal,
for lazy hands refuse to labor.
NRSVCE.26:
All day long the wicked covet,
but the righteous give and do not hold back.
NRSVCE.27:
The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination;
how much more when brought with evil intent.
NRSVCE.28:
A false witness will perish,
but a good listener will testify successfully.
NRSVCE.29:
The wicked put on a bold face,
but the upright give thought to their ways.
NRSVCE.30:
No wisdom, no understanding, no counsel,
can avail against the LORD.
NRSVCE.31:
The horse is made ready for the day of battle,
but the victory belongs to the LORD.

NET.1: The king’s heart is in the hand of the LORD like channels of water; he turns it wherever he wants.
NET.2: All of a person’s ways seem right in his own opinion, but the LORD evaluates the motives.
NET.3: To do righteousness and justice is more acceptable to the LORD than sacrifice.
NET.4: Haughty eyes and a proud heart — the agricultural product of the wicked is sin.
NET.5: The plans of the diligent lead only to plenty, but everyone who is hasty comes only to poverty.
NET.6: Making a fortune by a lying tongue is like a vapor driven back and forth; they seek death.
NET.7: The violence done by the wicked will drag them away because they refuse to do what is right.
NET.8: The way of the guilty person is devious, but as for the pure, his way is upright.
NET.9: It is better to live on a corner of the housetop than in a house in company with a quarrelsome wife.
NET.10: The appetite of the wicked desires evil; his neighbor is shown no favor in his eyes.
NET.11: When a scorner is punished, the naive becomes wise; when a wise person is instructed, he gains knowledge.
NET.12: The Righteous One considers the house of the wicked; he overthrows the wicked to their ruin.
NET.13: The one who shuts his ears to the cry of the poor, he too will cry out and will not be answered.
NET.14: A gift given in secret subdues anger, and a bribe given secretly subdues strong wrath.
NET.15: Doing justice brings joy to the righteous and terror to those who do evil.
NET.16: The one who wanders from the way of wisdom will end up in the company of the departed.
NET.17: The one who loves pleasure will be a poor person; whoever loves wine and anointing oil will not be rich.
NET.18: The wicked become a ransom for the righteous, and the faithless are taken in the place of the upright.
NET.19: It is better to live in a desert land than with a quarrelsome and easily-provoked woman.
NET.20: There is desirable treasure and olive oil in the dwelling of the wise, but a foolish person devours all he has.
NET.21: The one who pursues righteousness and love finds life, bounty, and honor.
NET.22: The wise person can scale the city of the mighty and bring down the stronghold in which they trust.
NET.23: The one who guards his mouth and his tongue keeps his life from troubles.
NET.24: A proud and arrogant person, whose name is “Scoffer,” acts with overbearing pride.
NET.25: What the sluggard desires will kill him, for his hands refuse to work.
NET.26: All day long he craves greedily, but the righteous gives and does not hold back.
NET.27: The wicked person’s sacrifice is an abomination; how much more when he brings it with evil intent!
NET.28: A lying witness will perish, but the one who reports accurately speaks forever.
NET.29: A wicked person shows boldness with his face, but as for the upright, he discerns his ways.
NET.30: There is no wisdom and there is no understanding, and there is no counsel against the LORD.
NET.31: A horse is prepared for the day of battle, but the victory is from the LORD.
